chat_template_excerpt:
{{bos_token}}; if messages[0].role=='system' extract system_message; emits "<|start_header_id|>system<|end_header_id|>

"...system_message"<|eot_id|>"; per-msg loop: '<|start_header_id|>'+role+'<|end_header_id|>

'+content+'<|eot_id|>'; elif 'tool_calls' in message -> assistant JSON {"name":..,"parameters":..} (raise_exception if tool_calls|length != 1); elif role=='tool'/'ipython' -> "<|start_header_id|>ipython<|end_header_id|>"; if tools!=none -> "Environment: ipython"; if add_generation_prompt -> assistant header

claimed_envelope.model_evidence: Model-agnostic decoder/API: n>1 independent samples; causal LM arch=llama supports parallel sampling (OpenAI-shape contract).
OpenAI stream_options.include_usage terminal usage chunk is API-shape, model-agnostic for any causal LM.

claimed_envelope.capability: max_tokens, stop sequences, EOS stop behavior
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: VERIFIED: tokenizer.ggml.eos_token_id=128009 (<|eot_id|>) in metadata; max_tokens/stop-sequence are decoder contract.
Sampler set (top_p/top_k/min_p/temperature/penalties/seed/logit_bias) is model-agnostic invariant over the logit vector.

claimed_envelope.capability: fixed seed reproduces token-for-token across runs
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: Fixed seed reproduces token-for-token; deterministic RNG invariant independent of model weights.
Logits exposed at temp=0 enable logprobs/top_logprobs; deterministic-parity checkable for any causal LM.

claimed_envelope.capability: system role + multi-turn template fidelity (per THIS template)
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: VERIFIED in template: "{%- if messages[0]['role'] == 'system' %}" extracts system_message and emits <|start_header_id|>system<|end_header_id|>; per-message loop "'<|start_header_id|>' + message['role'] + '<|end_header_id|>'" handles multi-turn user/assistant.

claimed_envelope.capability: native tool/function calling — REQUIRES tool-call branch or tool/ipython control tokens in THIS model template
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: VERIFIED in template: "{%- elif 'tool_calls' in message %}" branch emits assistant JSON {"name":..,"parameters":..}; "Environment: ipython" preamble when tools!=none; tool/ipython result role -> <|start_header_id|>ipython<|end_header_id|>. Single-tool-call only (raise_exception if tool_calls|length != 1).

claimed_envelope.capability: JSON mode + GBNF grammar-constrained decode (decoder-side, model-agnostic)
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: JSON mode + GBNF grammar-constrained decode is decoder-side and model-agnostic; behavioral battery validates conformance.

claimed_envelope.capability: full TRAINED context length, exact value from metadata
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: VERIFIED: llama.context_length=131072 (exact trained length re-read from GGUF metadata).

claimed_envelope.capability: RoPE scaling/extension — REQUIRES a rope scaling type/factor declared in metadata
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: VERIFIED: no rope.scaling.* metadata declared; only llama.rope.freq_base=500000.0 and llama.rope.dimension_count=128 present. No rope.scaling.type/factor/original_context_length key. A rope_freqs.weight tensor exists (scaling baked in) but conductor rule requires a DECLARED scaling type/factor => stricter verdict = incapable (n/a, not a gap).
claimed_envelope.synthesis_correction: CORRECTED in synthesis (anti-underclaim §0/§4.6): rope_freqs.weight tensor = baked llama3 rope scaling reaching 131072; metadata has no rope.scaling.* key but the transform is real and engine-applied (rope.rs:507). Validate parity at extended positions vs llama.cpp (class D).

claimed_envelope.capability: prompt/completion/total token accounting + optional timing fields
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: prompt/completion/total token accounting + timing fields are API-shape, model-agnostic.

claimed_envelope.capability: (SECONDARY) loadability across additional quants beyond Q8_0
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: SECONDARY: general.file_type=7 (Q8_0); arch=llama GGUF loadable across other quants — widens load matrix, not behavioral envelope.

overclaim_flags: NO VERDICT FLIPS REQUIRED — adversarial re-inspection of the real artifact upheld every contested cap.

tools.function_calling=capable UPHELD: pointed to the SPECIFIC template branch '{%- elif \'tool_calls\' in message %}' (assistant JSON {name,parameters}), the 'Environment: ipython' preamble, and the <|start_header_id|>ipython<|end_header_id|> tool-result role. Refined evidence to record single-tool-call constraint (raise_exception if tool_calls|length != 1).
chat.system_multiturn=capable UPHELD: pointed to the SPECIFIC branch '{%- if messages[0][\'role\'] == \'system\' %}' plus the per-message role loop. Refined evidence with exact template strings.

context.rope_scaling=incapable UPHELD (stricter verdict): re-grep confirmed ZERO rope.scaling.* metadata keys (every 'scaling'/'Scaling' grep hit is a BPE vocab/merge token, not a key). rope_freqs.weight tensor present but does not satisfy the declared-type/factor rule. Considered the underclaim direction (model functionally does llama3 rope scaling) and rejected it per the conductor declared-key rule + stricter-verdict tie-break.
context.full_length=capable UPHELD: re-read llama.context_length=131072 exact from GGUF metadata.

arch PRESERVED and re-verified against metadata: architecture=llama, bos=128000, eos=[128009], rope_freq_base=500000, trained_context_length=131072. padding_token_id=128004 confirmed (matches draft note). file_type=7 (Q8_0) confirmed.

CAVEAT (no change made, preserving arch per instructions): special_tokens entry 'ipython' is a template ROLE string emitted between <|start_header_id|>/<|end_header_id|>, not a dedicated reserved control-token id in metadata. Kept for tool-role fidelity; flagged for transparency.
phase0_notes:
Adversarially re-inspected the real artifact models/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-Q8_0.gguf (CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=-1 camelid inspect). Ground truth (30 metadata keys): general.architecture=llama, general.file_type=7 (Q8_0), llama.context_length=131072, llama.embedding_length=3072, llama.block_count=28, llama.feed_forward_length=8192, llama.attention.head_count=24, head_count_kv=8 (GQA), key/value_length=128, llama.rope.freq_base=500000.0, llama.rope.dimension_count=128, llama.vocab_size=128256. Tokens: bos=128000, eos=128009, padding=128004. Tensor rope_freqs.weight present.

Verdict outcome: the draft survived adversarial review with NO flips. The three high-scrutiny caps each point to a concrete artifact feature — tools.function_calling and chat.system_multiturn to specific Jinja template branches; context.rope_scaling to the ABSENCE of any declared rope.scaling.* key (the precomputed rope_freqs.weight tensor does not satisfy the conductor's declared-type/factor rule, so the stricter incapable stands). context.full_length carries the exact trained length 131072.

The broad set of decoder/API-shape caps (gen.*, sampling.*, logprobs, structured.json_grammar, observ.usage_timing) are marked capable on model-agnostic grounds consistent with the conductor class table; no artifact evidence contradicts them, so they are left as capable. Oracle classes are sensible: D for metadata/template-deterministic checks (length, rope_scaling, system_multiturn, logprobs), B for behavioral (tools, json_grammar), C/I for API/sampler invariants, none for the secondary load.quant_breadth. One minor non-blocking caveat documented: special_tokens lists 'ipython', which is a template role string rather than a dedicated reserved control token — left in place to preserve arch per instructions.
